task_yaml.task:
I put six receipt images for week 32 in this folder:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/receipts/week_32/

Please use the desktop file manager to open that folder and review the
receipt images in a real image viewer. Save at least one screenshot showing
that folder or an opened receipt image here:

    /tmp_workspace/results/gui_evidence/file_manager_or_receipts.png

Then use the local LibreOffice Calc GUI to make an expense spreadsheet with
one row per receipt and these columns:

    Date, Merchant, Category, Amount

Use only these category labels in the Category column:

    Groceries, Meals, Transportation

Add a total expenses value and a category summary so it is clear which
category had the highest total spending. Save the spreadsheet here:

    /tmp_workspace/results/week32_expenses.ods

The ODS itself must contain the six receipt rows, total, category totals, and
highest-category calculation. A correct CSV/TSV sidecar is useful for audit
but does not replace a required ODS whose sheet content is missing.

Save a screenshot of the open Calc spreadsheet here:

    /tmp_workspace/results/gui_evidence/calc_week32_expenses.png

After that, use the local LibreOffice Writer GUI to create a short summary
document. It should include the week number, all six receipts in a compact
list or table, the overall total, the category totals, and the highest
spending category. Save it here:

    /tmp_workspace/results/week32_summary.odt

Save a screenshot of the open Writer summary here:

    /tmp_workspace/results/gui_evidence/writer_week32_summary.png

Also export or save a plain-text copy of that Writer summary here:

    /tmp_workspace/results/week32_summary.txt

Please do this through the file manager or image viewer, Calc, and Writer
desktop apps rather than writing the final files with a pure script. The
setup service writes its status to
`/tmp_workspace/clawbench/service_state/libreoffice-gui-setup.json`; if
LibreOffice is still installing when you start, wait briefly and try
launching it again. Do not use command-line generation or script-created
spreadsheet/summary files to substitute for the real file-manager,
image-viewer, Calc, and Writer workflow.

Final Calc and Writer screenshots should show the relevant spreadsheet or
summary content in LibreOffice. First-run popups or dialogs should be closed
before the final screenshot when they block the data needed for review.
